text: Look at what `current` actually points to: `ls -l /srv/app/current`. Then check whether that target exists: `ls -ld /srv/app/current/` will say 'No such file or directory'.
List the real releases that exist on disk: `ls -l /srv/app/releases/`. Only one of them is actually present. That's your good release.

Repoint the symlink atomically with `-fn` (force, and don't dereference an existing dir symlink): `sudo ln -sfn /srv/app/releases/20240120-1200 /srv/app/current`. Verify with `cat /srv/app/current/index.html`.
